title: hypolepis rugosula subsp. villosoviscida
intro: Hypolepis rugosula subsp. villosoviscida, also known as the Rough Spleenwort, is a subspecies of fern that belongs to the genus Hypolepis, characterized by its distinctive, leathery fronds. The Rough Spleenwort has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic effects. The health benefits of this plant are attributed to its ability to reduce inflammation, soothe digestive issues, and exhibit antioxidant properties, making it a valuable remedy for various health conditions. The herbal preparations of Hypolepis rugosula subsp. villosoviscida include teas, infusions, and poultices, which can be used to treat a range of ailments, from skin irritations to gastrointestinal issues.
